channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: It’s all falling apart — 128 replies — 415.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
It’s all falling apart

Open thread • Replies: 128 • Updated: 2026-04-05 18:39 UTC

OP

At the same time. Bonds dumping, oil spiking, equities crashing

Trump is gonna go down as a worse version of Hoover + LBJ lol

Early replies (first 3)

62068038
>>>/pol/
Trump is not a king
Neither is he responsible for 80 years of BoomerX fuck ups

62068047
>>62068035
it's over

62068065
>>62068038

But he is the closest American president to a king, and the reason we are in this shitshow now. Spiking oil from 60 to 120 in a month is going to rally yields which are going to tighten financial conditions across the country

Tariffs are managed by the legislative, not the executive, but they didn’t stop king Cheeto from wasting a year and dumping markets 20% for being wrong

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62070088
>>62068437
Amen

62070189
>>62069671

bootlickers like this suck the best cock

62070263
>>62070189
[shill blank]
lol

62070285
The AOC 49 state sweep is going to be a brutal redpill for this country.

62070374
>>62070285
Just because the right is mad at Trump does not mean that they're suddenly going to vote for democrats.

62070388
>>62070374
correct. That's the funny part about demrat shills. They are currently dumping Billions in dollars into eroding the trust in the "state" on such a fundamental level and expecting for some reason that it is going to give them voter potential, when in fact it is just priming the population to go complete 1789 on the entire polity, no matter what flag they fly

62070448
>>62068035
that was always the point, distracting you with manufactured culture wars and financed talking heads while the establishment pulls the plug amidst managed chaos

thanks for playing

62071104
>>62068148
>tariffs (power of purse to congress)
delegated to the president centuries ago
>Iran war (power to declare war to congress)
Delegated to the president nearly a century ago, but the Iraq war involves the Navy and Air Forces, neither of which have ever been subject to congressional declarations of war. The first two barbary wars were fought by the US Navy without a declaration of war by congress, and literally no peeps were made about their constitutionality *by the people who wrote the constitution*. So fuck off with your bullshit.
>threatens to sue political opponents
Oh no not the president that was literally sued by his political opponents! How very gauche of him.
>threatens to sue federal reserve for not lowering rates like we are some 3rd world shithole like turkey
The federal reserve is a patently unconstitutional breach of constutitional executive authority. He should just shut it down.
>fights Supreme Court on tariffs and refuses to refund despite them saying he’s wrong
Separation of powers, bitch. The USSC is not a superior branch to the Administrative.
>uses his military police to literally kill American citizens (ICE)
Oh no police have never killed american citizens before. Perhaps they shouldn't have attempted to murder the ICE officers.
>Donald J. Trump signed 252 Executive orders between 2025 and 2026.
So you're seriously claiming that the president cannot direct the operations of his own executive branch? Because that's what an executive order is, it's an order to the executive branch. That's all it is. It isn't legislation, and you're a fucking retard for thinking it is, or you are just being a lying piece of shit who thinks we are all fucking retards that will fall for it.

62071127
>>62068374
The terms of Article 5 are satisfied by hopes and prayers and warm thoughts. Nobody would care if Estonia did it, but if the US does it then NATO effectively ceases to exist. Oh the piece of paper that the treaty is written on would still exist, and the French and Germans would parade around with their 6 working tanks and half an infantry division between them, for all anybody cares.

62071151
>>62068035
it's just money. they've got a money printer they use regularly for this.

62071160
>>62070448
absolutely. boomer wealth was always going to be fleeced. always.

62071162
>>62070374
They won't vote Democrat. They will vote AOC 2028

62071169
>>62068035
ever!
impossible!
earth shattering!
people asking for their money back is a revolution on par with spitting of the atom.

62071178
>>62071162
Go back to to your shitcord /tv/ clown shill

62071284
>>62068038
Trump was born in 1946 and has been fucking up our society since the beginning with the rest of the boomers. 1946 is the literal leading edge of the boomer generation, retard.

62071296
>>62071162
Unironically Tulsi 2028 if she ends up getting fired.

62071370
>>62071284
Sure, he is a Boomer, and that qualifies him to hang on the same noose BUT it does not mean that the rest of the BoomerX and more important the rest of the polity, bureaucracy, subsidized privates, bankers.... can hide behind him and memory hole themselves out of their guilt. He is not the scapegoat alone, that goes to everyone over 50 in the political sphere and close to it over the past 70 years, no exceptions

62072176
if you actually have any conviction try shorting blue owl, i’ll be happy to have you pay my dividend in may with your WORTHLESS short position.

62072205
>>62068713
>left liberal communist
schizo word salad

62072344
>>62068038
>BoomerX

Is it just the one guy pushing this term?

62073037
>>62068065
>the closest American president to a king
That had been Obama so far by a margin.

62073045
>>62068038
Trump is a boomer and his love for Israel has put the final nail in the US coffin

62075826
>>62068035
good.

62076650
>>62073037
Your png says that was bush jr not Obama, you fucking idiot

62076688
>>62072344
I think so.

62079321
>>62068148
So he's had fewer executive orders than his predecessors.
252 is smaller than 291 or 277

62079332
>>62069197
I thought Trump left COVID response to the individual states. So the lockdowns were more strict in New York and California then they were in Florida and Texas. And it was the governors of these states deciding the policies.

62079340
>>62068035
Am I safe in USDC?

62079733
>>62079332
>Trump left COVID response to the individual states.
He actually had no choice in the matter. He wanted the dems to open up hence he was screaming about the economy and opening up every day.

62079745
>>62073037
>Donny made 252 executive orders on his second term, in a fucking year. That totals to 472 while still having 3 years left (potentially if he survives)

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-04-05 18:26 UTC

OP

remember when linkies had hope. now all they have is PAIN. lmao and don't forget the misery. dabble on a little humiliation as well. and last but not least: incel rage hahahahhahaah

Early replies (first 3)

62015997

62016663

62016952

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62050929
everybody HATES STIIINKIIIIIIES hahahha

62050936
>>62045180
That'd be the end. But until then: 

62051982


62054375
fuck em all so much
hate em all think they're dumb so gay too
hate all stinks
fuckin cringe ass niggas

62054396
>>62054375

62054429
>>62015802
I remember the Summer of Dreams like it was yesterday.

62054451
>>62054429
>The world was at my fingertips!
>Waking up every day feeling high and mighty
We got too cocky linkmarines! HAHHAHAHAHAHAHAHHAHAA

62054546
>>62045180
bro , link didnt even break ath this cycle. there is no hope for link. cant even ride up with bitcin, founders atill dump millions. ita only going to go lower.

not even trying to fud. no clarity act wont increase hype. and no banks are not trying to surpress price.

there is no hype and no demand. no community ,

62054560
Even the AIs have hope now

62054650
>>62015802
I admire your dedication OP. The amount of eternally poor linkfag seethe that you have generated is more than all the twitter fudders put together. Respect.

62054880
>>62054560
cisco is a good comparison, except it's like 2005 instead of 1996.
just 20 more years until we get back to all time high!

62055022
soon all stinkies will be so poor they won't even know what's going on anymore. they'll be like: wtf, where'd my money gooooo lmao lmao LOL LOL lmao

62057405
i'll never ever ever stop laughin at stuuupid stiiiinks. hate em alllll
think they suuuuuuck

62057472
>>62015802
>>62015997
linkedin<ko-fi and patreon

62059066
>>62055022
Checked.
Except, they'll know exactly where their money went. The regrets are gonna haunt them up to the day they rope

62060805
>>62054560
alpha like this being openly posted daily is why ill have no sympathy for nolinkers in the future
>b...but we didnt know
kek

62061283
KABOOOOOOOOM

LINK

62062571
>>62060805
>AI slop is le alpha
I'm convinced these posts are some kind of bait
there's no way people that stupid actually exist

62063638
pain & misery

62066641
fuck em all

62067896
this is brutal how do they even cope? buy silver its about to moon

62070028
Fuck all the cuckolds who spammed /biz/ for 9 years

LMAOOOOO

62070732
hate link
simple as

62072180
fuck em all
truke

62073448
cuckoldry

62076967
>>62015802
Luckily, I dumped it for BTC and staked it on Babylon. Although the price is back to when I just started staking, it's still better than holding LINK

62078207
fuck all stinks
loathe all stinks
despise all stinks
detest all stinks

62079414
>>62015802
still never met a linky

62079722
>>62079414
count yourself lucky
they are among the world's most disgusting creatures

item.title: THIS IS ALL A CONFIRMED SETUP FOR TACO ON MONDAY BEFORE MARKET OPENS! — 17 replies — 68.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
THIS IS ALL A CONFIRMED SETUP FOR TACO ON MONDAY BEFORE MARKET OPENS!

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-04-05 18:50 UTC

OP

THIS IS ALL A CONFIRMED SETUP FOR TACO ON MONDAY BEFORE MARKET OPENS! HUGE "DEAL" WITH IRAN WILL BE ANNOUNCED (LIKE EVERY MONDAY)

Early replies (first 3)

62079115
>>62079101
big if true

62079132
trump fatigue

62079136
>>62079101

>Sent Kurds with guns to protesters

More like the CIA astroturfed and supplied weapons

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62079146
>>62079101
>Open the straight that I just said wasn't closed

62079151
>>62079101

Here's the thing though - is there anyone left to fall for it for what, the third time now? There probably won't be any pumps on monday there way there were no dumps this weekend because everyone expects the orange buffoon to continue kicking the can down the road

62079153
>>62079146
this is all so schizophrenic
and probably intentionally schizophrenic

62079172
>>62079151
Doesnt matter if anyone falls for it. Trump is gonna taco then everyone will buy because they think everyone else will buy. The S&P will close green on monday

62079215
aaaaaaaaaa

62079259
>>62079101
*takes bibis oil in his mouth

62079276
>>62079101
That's how he keeps doing it: making up something really scary that's going to happen that is purely imaginary. Then announce that the threat of the really scary thing is over (actually it never existed), and everyone feels joyful and exuberant again.

62079284
>>62079276
its all true though, we have been blowing up their power plants, bridges, a nuclear power plant, and even blew up their biggest oil well the other day.

62079298
Actually it's Tuesday.

62079322
>>62079284
We blew up the Bushehr nuclear plant on April 4, the B1 bridge in Karaj, and a major petrochemical hubs in Khuzestan and Kuwait

62079330
The nuclear power plant we blew up could fuck the UAE due to how the weather system works. Iran is protected by the mountains, everything will flow to the UAE. Major potential black swan desu senpai

62079346
>>62079172
>everyone will buy because they think everyone else will buy

Everyone looking to do this is after a quick pump and dump in the span of a few days, there's no actual "longs" here. Trump was spouting fire and brimstone over the weekend and nobody reacted so why should monday's "productive talks" be any different? Like with bitcoin cycles you can look at the past three weeks of market manipulation and the monday pumps have been getting weaker and weaker every time.

62079471
>>62079136
There is a strong CIA presence in Iraqi Kurdistan.

62079773
>>62079471
There must be a strong psychiatric presence in mar al alago desu.

62076912

62076916
>>62076906
he's right though. The Scrum Masters are in trouble, so we need an emergency TACO and bailout.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62076917
>>62076912
Because he'll make up some bullshit about how Iran and the US made amazing progress with peace talks. He did the exact same thing two weeks ago on monday

62076919

I would not be surprised.

62076920

The funny thing is nobody fucking remembered that lmao because he made up 10 more lies since then

62076922

Wake me up when they start carpet bombing Shiraz.

62076980
>>62076916
scrum master is some borat shit fakest job I ever heard of lmao

62077003
>>62076917
Iran has been getting pummeled all weeked. You retards act like nothing is being done.

62077004
>>62076906
Iran isn't gonna taco though. Why should they when they're winning. Keep it up until Israel is wrecked then the real run can begin.

62077041

62077042
>>62076906

62077047

>crypto didnt even blink

item.title: Financially Fucked By My Father — 16 replies — 34.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Financially Fucked By My Father

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-04-05 18:30 UTC

OP

My dad has no life insurance. Still owes $75k on the mortgage with 8 years to go. He only has $5k in the bank and $5k in his 401k at 63 years old.

He doesn't do ANY maintenance on the house. The basement hasn't had a dehumidifier in it for 7 years and it's full of rat shit and probably mold. The roof is overdue by 2 years and it'll cost about $20k for a new roof. Water collects around the foundation of the house when it rains and he doesn't do anything about it. The water filters need to be changed twice a month, but he only does it once a month, so there's rust in both bathrooms. The master bathroom bathtub drain leaks into the basement and he hasn't fixed it in 4 years, so he just showers in the other bathroom. The kitchen sink leaks water. The fridge is brown from dirt and grime. The stove is covered in dirt and grime. The microwave above the stove is broken, so he has a separate microwave on the counter. The carpet is 20 years old and destroyed. The laminate flooring is peeling away. The window sills are bubbled and peeled from water damage. The furnace is 20 years old. The garage has no electricity running to it and the garage door is broken. He doesn't even fix that when he used to install and repair garage doors as a job for 30 years. There are cobwebs in the living areas. The porches are falling apart and it's a tripping hazard every time you walk up the stairs.

All he does is get drunk, jackoff to porn, blast music and mow the lawn.

Having a parent that is lazy, financially retarded and unhygienic will screw you over when it comes to owning and maintaining a home. I refuse to put $100k into the house to make it enjoyable to live in, especially when he's still living there.

My mom is awesome though and she lives with me.

Are you in the same situation, or did you get lucky enough to have intelligent, hardworking parents that actually care about your future finances?

Early replies (first 3)

62078756
>>62078722
so sound like a faggot

62078808
>insure house alot
>fire
>profit

62078820
>>62078722
Not really seeing how your dad's financial issues affect you at all though, much less how you are "financially fucked" by this.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62078843
Get off your lazy pasty cracka ass and do it yo goddamn self. Your dad is probably like 70

62078854
>All he does is get drunk, jackoff to porn, blast music and mow the lawn.

Based?

62078870
>>62078820
>what is generational wealth
Classic retard.

62078891
>>62078870
Thank you. I didn't feel like replying to something that stupid.

62078925
my grandad bought my dad a business which he ran into the ground. then he sold it when he was 50 and has been 'retired' since - he's 80 now.

62078942
>>62078925
actually i just remembered, he actually ran the first business into the ground, sold it, then started a new one with the money and then ran the second one into the ground and then 'retired'
generational run t bh

62078968
>>62078870
Your dad doesn't owe you generational wealth though. His failure to gift you millions of dollars does not in any way constitute "fucking you over financially". It is his house, to do with as he pleases, and until and unless he wills it to you, it is of no business to you, you entitled little shit.

62079025
>>62078968
So true king, fuck family they dont deserve shit from you.

62079045
>>62079025
Not once they hit 18 they don't. Also I don't know if you were intending to come across like a whiny little bitch, but you did an excellent job of it.

62079085
>>62078870
I feel bad for people who fall for this meme, nothing lasts. Unless you're a tech lord that money will probably be gone in one or two generations, even if you're a tech lord. Caring what your father has is peak beta shit.

62079105
>>62079045
I did intend to come off as a whiny bitch, I talk like that and act like that and I look like a whiny little bitch.

62079725
at least you weren't literally fucked by your father like me

62079728
>>62078722
fuck off retard. its his money and his life. he can do with HIS property what he wants. you are entitled to NOTHING. I would burn my house down to the ground if I had a son like you.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 252 replies — 926.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 252 • Updated: 2026-04-05 18:41 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62059225
DOT will reach the $1.60 range before this week ends

62059268
>>62057110
See was that so hard on being influential?

62060540
>>62059268
The thing is, that "influenced" you... Doesn't mean it influenced the other people. Everyone is a unique little program and therefore needs unique inputs in order to produce the desired output. One man's trash is another man's treasure

>>62058962
Nagger

62060621
100$ end of the FUCKING bull run. Here's some influential and intellectual insights

62060648
>>62060540
How do you know. Besides the intent of you making this thread was to educate/shill/ influence the mass retards on this site, don't try to convince me otherwise

62062169
>>62060648
>How do you know.
What do you mean by that? How do I know what?

62062500
>>62060648
How I know that people are unique little programs and that I need to input unique inputs to get the desired outputs? Is that what you're asking or?

62062550
Let me "influence" you young Vishnus of the MINDchain. Listen to one of our spiritual leaders Oliver Talé-Yaźdi how he talks about the Vishnu mindchain. Join our cult *kmh* *kmh* web3 organization where we talk about the new world order.

https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2039256751346700502

62065568
100$ end of MIND RUN

62068446
12 more hours and the thread will be alive for 30 days and that's when Gavin Wood gives me 11.5k DOT for my "influencing" of your young Vishnus of the mindchain.

62068604
i buy this shit at 6.6$ or -75% from ATH
NOW I M AT -90% DOWN

62068658
>>62068604
yeah the decline of this shit has been remarkable. and it still somehow has a $2 billion market cap.

62069192
>>62068658
im very disappointed

62069282
>>62069192
Good. Now sell so I can GIGA short it some more while shilling it on 4chan. You activated my trap card anon "Backstabbing shill" (Effect: Convince people DOT is THE SOLUTION while having a giga short open at the same time).

62071796
100$ end of bull run. And it's official, the thread has stayed alive for 30 days and therefore Gavin Wood will pay me 11.5k$. That will feed Mumbai for literally a whole year.

Thank you Vishnu

62072073
>>62071796
You're welcome.

Don't I get a little something

62073144
>>62072073
But of course Panjali, your are a citizen of Mumbai city, the city of DOT. Every citizen gets paid. We all get feed and loved by our kind lord Gavin Wood. He is the underworld CP kingpin. He sells Cheese Pizza and then redistributes the funds among DOT holders. Everyone in Mumbai is eating and licking Cheese Pizza. I also like to put some cream onto my Cheese Pizza and then lick it off.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62075463
100$ end of bull run

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62077075
Every day after the 30th day I get 150 DOT as a bonus. If we keep the thread alive for 30 more days I'll get 30 * 150 = 4500 more DOT. Please help contribute so my village of Mumbai gets feed.

62078039
>>62077075
You are not in India

62078216
>>62078039
This is my hand >>62008295 young Vishnu. This was taken directly from Mumbai, the city of DOT.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62078593
Dont selling my dotty anymore

62078595
75

62078631
Depressing thread

62078789
>>62078216
Fake

62079572
>>62078631
Well, I've already got my 11.5k DOT that Gaving promised me and every day this thread stay alive I get an additional 150DOT. Doesn't really seem depressing to me

>>62078593
>>62078595
100$ END OF BULL RUN

>>62078789
I am brown as chocolate itself. I am a true Mumbai warrior

Thank you for keeping the thread alive my Mumbai warriors. You are feeding your village. This is a cooperative effort. The mumbai boys strike AGAIN


Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62079598
This is the most damaging thread for dot in history

62079614
>>62079598
Read this >>62069282. We're playing this whole board. They're eating right out of our hands. First we make them believe that DOT is held by the GLAMOROUS citizens of Mumbai. Then at the same time we pocket the money from Gavin Wood for keeping the thread alive. AND at the same time we SHORT IT TO 0$. The whole city of Mumbai is in on it. INDIA SUPER POWER BY 2020 wasn't a lie. It's a FACT for 6 years already.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62079649
>>62079614
But is the tech good?

62079750
>>62079649
Literally THE BEST chain currently. And no, I'm not just making shit up. It literally does what everyone else is trying to do. Once JAM + NOMT hit it'll be able to do MILLIONS of TPS (not just value tx) while doing continous compute, while being 100% decentalized, while the tx will be CHEAP, while having NATIVE interoperability, while being able to control other chains and never leave DOT, ... This projects is THE project and people are still buying meme coins. It's UNFATHOMABLE.

item.title: Should I buy more BTC or altcoins? — 57 replies — 29.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Should I buy more BTC or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 57 • Updated: 2026-04-05 17:01 UTC

OP

BTC is steady and battle-tested. Altcoins can go up more but are riskier.

>You buying more BTC like Saylor does or alts like ETH and SOL this cycle?

Early replies (first 3)

62059768
I'm going all in on SOL

62059809
>>62059007
Allocating a small portion to CreditScoreProtocol * com can generate substantial wealth

62061375
>>62059768

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62069260
>>62067554
It’s a lot better than just holding your BTC with no return. More people should explore how BTCFi works now.

62069267
>>62065091
You're just doing the right thing anon.

62070210
>>62061834

62071949
>>62069260
IF YOU'RE TALKING DEFI. ID GO FOR ETHEREUM AND SOLANA

62071968
Pls stop saylor. Stop

62071975
>>62067166
Do it eyyyyyy

62072569
>>62071949
Yeah ETH and Solana are solid, but only ~1% of BTC is in DeFi. Lots to explore, like Trustless Bitcoin Vaults, which will let BTC earn yield, be used as collateral, or participate in lending. That’s just getting started anon.

62072572
>>62065610

62072782
>>62059007

62072785
>>62067204
zero

62072829
>>62065610

62073188
>>62065656

62073628
>>62072782

62073641
>>62059007
if you guys could buy as much bitcoin as you can, driving the price up to and over $96,000 so that I can make my exit, that would be great. thanks a bunch.

62073652
>>62073641
You're just coward to hold and wait till 5 years

62073673
>>62072569

62073687
>>62073641

62075889
>>62066497
I dont know. Maybe your trump coin will lol

62076395
>>62059007

62076433
>>62059007
BTC or nothing, smart anons are staking and just waiting for the pump.

62076441
>>62067554
>>62071949
ignore the jeet shilling his staking btc scam. He shills it in every thread.
Are you guys really that stupid to not realize this? The jeet was shilling some privacy defi scam before, forgot the name. It's probably some LLM bot tbqh

62076475
BTC will hit $70000 before the end of today, buy now.

Trust me.

62076605
>>62076441

62076963
>>62072569

62078281
>>62076475

62078766

62078785

62079466

item.title: The US dollars was 70% of the world's reserve currency in 2016. — 63 replies — 25.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US dollars was 70% of the world's reserve currency in 2016.

Open thread • Replies: 63 • Updated: 2026-04-05 16:26 UTC

OP

The US dollars was 70% of the world's reserve currency in 2016. It's 39% now.

Early replies (first 3)

62074981
>>62074965
lol no, the financial bust part is the great depression, the revolution and wars is the period between the end of ww2 and 2008, debt and restructuring was 2008 onwards

62074987
Seems like a silly unfounded chart given that gold were the main reserves before the dollar.

62075007
>>62074981
>the revolution and wars is the period between the end of ww2 and 1971, debt and restructuring was 1971 to 2008(2020 maybe?), new world order from then on

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62075752
>>62074981
>financial bust part is the great depression
You're right but that was the last cycle not the current one. We already had a new world order after the great depression and that world is quickly coming to an end. The upside for those prepared is a rare opportunity to enter into the upper echelons.

62075758
>>62075725
Batteries are okay for a consumer market. It ensures a demand BECASUE those shit devices die fairly regular and fast. You don't run an industry on fucking batteries

62075825
>>62075745
That's ok buddy you can continue your autistic belief that mountains conquer markets

62075897
>>62075140
Don’t bother, these negative retards can’t understand the strength of soft power.

62075904
>>62075286
>overt negativity and a weird positivity towards russia

Kek some people can’t help but to be contrarian.

62075906
>>62075897
EU is filled with infighting and requiring a consensus, there's no way it can be a power.

62075932
>>62075906
Europeans now understand that either we work together or face irrelevancy and being raped by other powers. There will be a lot less infighting in the future.

62076103
>>62075752
How do you prepare?

62076183
>>62075897
What soft power?

62076222
>>62074965
I voted for this

62076517
>>62075745
Jesus you are really retarded. You know batteries can be recycled right??

62076522
>>62076103
buy chainlink

62076525
>>62075897
Soft power only works when hard power is behind it.

62076528
>>62075897
>soft power of EU
South Korea has more soft power than all the EU countries combined. And if they unite with NK, they'll have more hard power too

62076535
>>62074965
Get rid. Sling bastard hook.

62076539
>>62076528
>South Korea has more soft power than all the EU countries combined
Lol.

62076546
>>62076539
It is funny that this is the state of EU isn't it

62076552
>>62075131
>>62075717
Right wing at this point means traitor as you can see at hand of these two negative morons.

62076603
>>62076546
There are people all over the planet speaking English and French, for one example. Let's count how many countries South Korea can say the same for. I'll start.

1. South Korea.

62077033
>>62076103
Get a decent amount of capital and store it in safe assets then when there is a major crash you buy quality companies at a steep discount.

62077390
>>62076517
>please believe my scam
>an industrialized territory with electricity demand in the Terra Watts is totally going to run on batteries and solar
>y-you can recycle the material from LIBs
AHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHAHAHAHAHAHHAHAHAHAHHAHAHAHAHHAHAHHAHAHAHAHHAHAHHAHAH

62077408
>>62076552
>still trying to run the "right wing" left libtardian etatist scapegoat
Did it work over the past 20 years or did it ironically had a Streisand effect to call everyone that isnt your close etatistic epistemic community a "national SOCIALIS 'right winger' "

Yeah thought so, keep a cyanide capsule close to you at all time, in July the first bond auctions fail in probably France, Italy or Japan and the collapse is going to end your fantasy

62077415
>>62074987
And gold has now surpassed the dollar as international reserve asset.

62077421
>>62077415
and? A shovel to your head and your gold stack changed the owner. There is only one universal currency among humans, its violence and the state only has mercenaries, with the state as a concept being bankrupt.

Mercenaries are great in peace times, in times of stress they are the ones ending the Prince

62077847
>>62077390
>bro I learned about solar and batteries in the 80’s. It MUST be the same ok?

The absolute state

62077968
>>62077847
You can spew the marketing shit as much as you like, LIB+solar is not going to run an industrialized economy.
>b-but china
no. China is as good as dead if it doesn't either sucks uncle sams dick real good, or invades Siberia and Kamchatka

62078052
>>62076603
England isn't even in EU dumbo

>>62077421
The absolute delusion in this thread lmao

62078161
>>62077968
Nobody said it will be the only source of power. It will the cheapest and safest with the fossils as backups. Midwit.

62079317
>>62078161
>marketing fag paddling back
lol. That is equal to admit that you are full of shit.
China has two options, conquer Siberia and Kamchatka or suck uncle sams dick real good, which means buying up as much debt as uncle sam wants to shove down chinas throat

62079408
>>62074965
so don't hold cash?

item.title: Litecoin — 11 replies — 23.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Litecoin

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-04-05 18:29 UTC

OP

Shill me this coin. The price is already bobbling at the 2022 low unlike SOL an ZEC which still have a long way to go. But BTC has not finished its cycle and every freaking coin today is going along with this mf.

Early replies (first 3)

62077150
$4000 by 2027

62077343
>>62077019
Litecoin, stellar lumens and hbar are the holy trinity of eternal shitcoins.

62077548
>>62077019
It's always gonna be around and has a stable floor around $50. Worth slurping and hoping for the best

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62077772
>>62077019
Oh wow, using a physical silver coin to represent digital 1s and 0s trash instead of the stereotypical golden coin. At least that's something unique.

62078474
>>62077019
I like LTC, such a nice comfy coin
Almost no fees, very fast confirmations
Lots of places accept it (for crypto)
Just a really nice crypto coin, I don't know why the creator regrets making Litecoin

62078841
>>62077019
LTC has 3 things going on for it:
>real world usage
Unlike 99.9% of crypto projects people actually use it for transactions.
>oldfags positive sentiment
Everyone knows LTC and for most part even hardcore BTC maxis "tolerate" LTC or point to it as the least shit shitcoin. LTC is generally "liked", like dogecoin. Crypto is all vaporware driven by psychology so a solid, decade old positive sentiment is very rare. The network strength itself is kinda solid as well.
>legal classification
It has clear legal classification as a pure commodity because it wasn't premined, the creator is out of the picture and it's proof of work. I'm not sure if it's up to date but BTC and LTC were the only 2 PoW shitcoins with pure commodity legal status in the amerifats land.
>the tech
Absolutely irrelevant in 2026. There were some efforts to market it as a "private" silver to BTCs gold but nobody uses LTC for privacy. Nobody uses the NFTs on LTC.

The list of negatives is way bigger and more important.
>70% of coins owned by pajeet tier whales
>price is manipulated into kingdom fuck
>tech is irrelevant
>massive amount of bagholders

62078850
>>62078841
Tl;dr - in 2026 LTC is a shittier version of Dogecoin

62079657
>>62078841
Sold.

62079684
>>62078850
They're merge mined so their destiny is intertwined

62079697
litecoin is literally just bitcoin with a couple changes to the code

62079727
>>62079697
Better or worse?

item.title: So, now that assblaster was confirmed controlled opposition, and LINK holders have become "the Cuckolds of Crypto"... — 28 replies — 20.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
So, now that assblaster was confirmed controlled opposition, and LINK holders have become "the Cuckolds of Crypto"...

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-04-05 17:36 UTC

OP

So, now that assblaster was confirmed controlled opposition, and LINK holders have become "the Cuckolds of Crypto"... shouldn't we have a look at what he told us *not* to buy?

He tried very hard to steer us away from one coin in particular back in 2018.

They wanted you out.

Early replies (first 3)

62077049
well he was right about raiblocks

62077089
>>62077049
Only in the sense that he was "right" about LINK. It's price did go up, yet it achieved nothing. Sergey met Trump and still nobody cares about smartcontracts. LINK had every chance to succeed but failed.

>Controlled opposition is a political or strategic tactic where a governing body, organization, or elite group secretly supports, infiltrates, or manages its own opposition to manipulate dissent, control narratives, and prevent real challenges to the status quo

A functional stateless currency would challenge the status quo, and now it's been "over 5 years under the radar".

Watch how long this thread stays up.

62077098
>>62077000
>>62077049
>>62077089

62077101

i dont belive in the project but this shit is gonna go to 11 dollars easily in the next few months

BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62077423
>>62077416

62077648
Anybody have the ari juels posts from earlier this year?

62077784
>>62077648
Hey remember when the Solana founders posted all that cryptic shit before their coin melted faces? Remember when Vitalik left all those breadcrumbs for us right before ETHs supermoon?

Yea me fucking neither. Fuck all this Da Vinci code bullshit cuz all the real projects didnt need it. Link needs to either shit or get off the pot, im tired of waiting.

62077915
>>62077784

>>62077784

62078415
>>62077915

geeze you guys are schizos lmao

62078437
>>62077784
the project works as intended. blame the market participants not treating the coin like a stock. the issue is people don't hold longer than 24 hours anymore and only buy trending coins.

62078484
is XRB supposed to be XRP ?

62078586
>>62078484
Newfag

62078618
>>62077089
>yet it achieved nothing
Chainlink is among the very top in both crypto adoption and non-crypto adoption. By a very wide margin.

You're worryingly low iq.

62078620
>>62078618
The only thing that's "worryingly low" is LINK's price xD

The only thing its adopted are cuckolds online like you

62078663
>>62077915
Is Ari one of us?

62078703
HOLY SHIT

By sheer chance I happened to be watching Sunny today, "The gang finds a dumpster baby", and recognised one of the frames Asslarper used (pic related).

In this episode, Dennis (AB's avatar) convinces an activist to CHAINLINK himself to a tree, then LITERALLY KEKS him.

Now do you believe me?

Inverse Assblaster; what did he tell you was useless?

Raiblocks and Ripple.

Time is running out.

62078711
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62078735
He even admitted his intentions in one of his final posts.

This ARG goes DEEP.

62078857
>>62078703
everyone needs to see this

62078921
>>62078618

Or are you like a sportsball fan that pays the team regardless of outcome and don't share in the winnings?

62079599
>>62078703

item.title: Calling all high IQ anons (not political nuts) here — 25 replies — 10.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Calling all high IQ anons (not political nuts) here

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-04-05 16:38 UTC

OP

How does Trump's political actions affect crypto positively? There must be something positively to all of this

Early replies (first 3)

62067242
There's a bitcoin toll booth at the hormussy now

62067263
>>62067233
Negative:
Trump is causing all kinds of uncertainty with his erratic decisions on macroeconomic points like global tariffs and wars.
Crypto being a high risk asset, it absolutely eats shit on this aspect.

Positive:
He's pushing for more crypto-positive legislation, meaning institutional adoption is getting closer and closer.

The negative point hits the speculative side of crypto; which also happens to be the sole price mover in all of crypto's history.
The positive point benefits actual mass adoption, which has not happened yet in all of crypto's history.

So on the whole the positive should massively outweigh the negative.

62072495
>>62067233
ignore all externalities of news and politics, focus on PA and trade how it was intended

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62072514
>>62067233
You ask that question like most of his actions have been bullish.

There was a big narrative that everybody was hyping themselves over, and that was him firing Gensler and making the SEC more altcoin friendly, that was the second biggest narrative that rivaled Blackrock getting spot BTC ETF's. That's supposed to be bullish, but 2025 was meh for altcoins compared to the price action in 2024 when he wasn't even president yet.

62073189
>>62067233
Extremely positively.

Trump banned CDBCs, Central bank cryptos. Basically slave electronic money that would track you.
And allowed regular cryptos.
Now the main boomer in Swift who was blocking crypto and XRP got fired.

>Now That XRP is Dead, What’s Next? Swift Executive Calls Ripple a Dead Chain Walking
>sep 2 2025
https://99bitcoins.com/news/altcoins/now-that-xrp-is-dead-whats-next-swift-executive-calls-ripple-a-dead-chain-walking/

Got fired out of SWIFT , 4 3 2026, 6 months later.
Another boomer who was blocking crypto and xrp got removed.

62073192
>>62072514
>There was a big narrative that everybody was hyping themselves over, and that was him firing Gensler and making the SEC more altcoin friendly,
This.
Also Trump's new FED chair that will soon start is crypto friendly.

62073195
>>62073189
>Tom Zschach
>blocking crypto

you're retarded

62073199
>>62073189
>Got fired out of SWIFT , 4 3 2026, 6 months later.
Today's news:
Tom Zschach is Stepping down as CIO of Swift

62073203
>>62073195
>Now That XRP is Dead, What’s Next?
>Swift Executive Calls Ripple a Dead Chain Walking
>sep 2 2025
You can find more links to those news .

62073212
>>62073203
The news isn't what makes you retarded

62073214
>>62073212
Got you, your low iq "expert" opinion does.

62073217
>>62073214
Has nothing to do with my opinion either.

62073223
>>62073217
The OP asked only high iq smart people here,
and that's not you.

62073358
crypto is dead

62073512
>>62067233
Crypto is dead already. Quantum computing will hack Bitcoin in ~3 years and that will be that.

62073546
>>62067233
you’re a silly guy with your framing of the question toward a single outcome of positivity

62073550
>>62073189
>XRP
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62074303
>>62067233
>The US government takes over Venezuelan oil fields in the name of narco-terrorism.
>The staged war cuts off Gulf States' oil supply
>US will have a monopoly on energy
>Ceasefire
>Treasuries are sold
>USD is printed
>CLARITY act revisions with stablecoin yields encourage swaps to securities and commodities
>CLARITY act passes
>More ETFs are approved
>Large US financial firms, like Black Rock, tokenize RWAs
>Midterms

62075049
>>62067233
Correlation between BTC and gold is 0.85 ie its basically digital gold. Fiat currencies go up BTC will go down. I think BTC is a great hedge against a 2008 style crash, though not at the start when everyone just wants money. If US discredits the dollar more it could lose credence as the catalyst for international finance. Alternatives like BTC would do good.

62075726
>>62067263
What would mass adoption look like and will it ever happen?

62076494
Go hard in BTC or ETH. Other coins will exist and make people money but it won't be a market like gold/btc, more in the sense of aluminium or whatever.
The next generation uses crypto a lot, streamers are constantly pushing it and those niggers along with us can change it in to a more crypto adaptle world. Be the change nigga, don't wait for a handout. God favours the bold.

62076501
>>62073223
holy shit just shut the fuck up ya moron lmao

62076730
>>62067233
>How does Trump's political actions affect crypto positively?
it hasn't, yet. so far it's mostly been lip service. the only law passed so far has been the GENIUS act, which will kick in next year, but who knows what that will actually accomplish. everything else remains an unfulfilled promise

62076808
>>62076494
HYPE will absolutely mog your boomer couins for the simple reality that it's the best way to trade the stock market on Saturdays when Trump randomly decides to declare he's invading Kharg island and you aren't able to react normally to stock market moves because it's closed.

62079447
>>62067233
He normalized it.
Got millions of normies into the fold, average person understands what it is now

item.title: Did you guys see that just recently HEX became the 3rd most held token on all of Ethereum? — 17 replies — 10.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Did you guys see that just recently HEX became the 3rd most held token on all of Ethereum?

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-04-05 17:19 UTC

OP

Did you guys see that just recently HEX became the 3rd most held token on all of Ethereum?
Only behind USDC and Tether

Does this give it more legitimacy or will most see through this mass marketing dust campaign?
Either way, it's not cheap to send your coin to 5 million people in a shill campaign.....

Early replies (first 3)

62077138
>>62077135
its totally legit saar, ape in lifesavings!

62077151
read pic
I bca finding out which "wrong-talk" did I type that our janitor is malding over

62077172
>>62077151
Airdropping a fresh coin would mess everything up, so if you're going to fork it anyways, why not make a new chain where the ETH FagFoundation can't make another update that fucks it all up with higher fees?

I agree there is a lot of hate-marketing out there giving bad optics, so many twitter accounts hating and shitting on RH and his coins daily and you just know it's all fueled by rage over their bags they're down bad on

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62077902
fuck off heart you gangster fuck

62077984
>>62077902
how is it so hard to find him? hes literally in estonia and probably hits up the luxury stores once a week lol

62078130
>>62077984
When X enabled location info for profiles last year his account showed south east asia.

>>62077902
fake news, all assault charges have been dropped
it was quite possibly completely made up just to get him on the interpol list since you can't be on there for non-violent crimes.

62078173
>>62077135
What’s the difference between pHEX, eHEX and HEX in ethereum? I can’t tell you because idgaf I’m all in pDAI niggers

62078189
>>62077172
>why not make a new chain where the ETH FagFoundation can't make another update that fucks it all up with higher fees?
because you're overcomplicating the project which was supposed to be pure game-theory of stakers vs LPers/ traders

now you have a shitshow that sent this to a -99% death chart
it's dead because of it

>Airdropping a fresh coin would mess everything up
it would mess NOTHING up
hex was an airdrop to bitcoin holders in the first place

62078192
>>62078173
see
this is what I mean
now you have the pDAI shitshow
you have pHEX, eHEX, pulseX
it's a fucking circus

richard fucking failed because he didn't listen to his own advice

62078714
>>62078130
sure thing mr goldstein

62078715
>>62078130
>non-violent
rape is a violent crime

62078740
>>62078173
pDAI to Dorra.

62078749
>>62077135
>3rd most wash-traded coin on ethereum
Ftfya

62079415
>>62078740
>pDAI to Dorra.
Pulsechain needs its own native stabefuck. $1 is inevitable

62079565
>>62079415
>>62078800
>>62078740
#WARNING

The fork of DAI on Pulsechain is exploited, it was minted for FREE by malicious exploiters who use bots to try their best to shill this token. They minted millions of it for free and want others to buy it from them. Do not buy.

The shill campaign is completely out of narratives now, it's just brainless shill slop

item.title: *inhales* — 37 replies — 10.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
*inhales*

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-04-05 15:19 UTC

OP

BAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA OH LINKIES

Early replies (first 3)

62070715
Why would this be a bad thing for link?

62070725
>>62070715
well let me put it this way:
why would link EVER PUMP EVER AGAIN!!??!?!?!
fuckin stupid ass stinkbag

62070738
why did you crop out the full post?
>I'm not retiring. I saw something.
>I'm leaving to join a team of scientists and entrepreneurs building at the intersection of Frontier AI, scientific discovery and institutional finance. Researchers from Oxford, Harvard and Cambridge. Engineers from SpaceX, Google and Uber.
>More to come. But the reason I left the best job I've ever had is simple: this is what comes next and I had to join the team building it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62070843
>>62070819
good. we want you to suffer, stinkbag

62070876
>>62070843
I noticed.

62071007
>>62070876
how does it feel knowing your net worth will soon be negative, thanks to stink dumping :}

62071018
>>62071007
>negative net worth
How does it feel to be a double digit iq african?

62071025
>>62071018
>retard doesn't know how negatives work
>accuses others of being stupid
poetry :}

62071053
>>62071018
It’s absolutely possible to have negative net worth lmao

62071058
>>62070725
Jesus, what an embarrassing spaghetti spill. The absolute state of you.

62071080
>>62071058
>dude, memes from 90s rap songs
cringe :}

62071105
>>62070698
Thanks for keeping us updated on SWIFTs executives fellow anon

62071131
>>62070698
He should've just given Ripple a fair pass and he wouldn't have gotten shitcanned.

62071230
>>62070698
its funny how anyone who belibved in link abandons it sooner or later. hahaah

link was such a massive dissapointment.

i feel bad for all the loyal supporters of it.

62071340
Oh my

62071417
>>62071340
What does that even mean. Does this mean swift will be using xrp lol

62071423
>>62070715
you cant replace people, they said

62071524
>>62071417
they don't even know what certified means

62071543
>>62071423
Who said that? No one said that

62071620
>>62070698
It’s so over

62071927
>>62071620
For stinkies

62072238
>>62071417
In tumorweeks, evwrything can magically happen what will pump your centralized shittokens.

62072254
>>62071018
>avg cuckold HR lady vacation financier stinkie

62073502
so Kemal leaves chainlink to work on an ai project and then 2 years later tom leaves swift to work on ai project that " has Researchers from Oxford, Harvard and Cambridge. Engineers from SpaceX, Google and Uber." and i'm supposed to be nervous and sell my link? yea in your wildest dreams. NEVER SELLING. POOL IS CLOSED.

62073562
>>62071007
you're german and hold link

62073601
>>62070738
>why did you crop out the full post?
>>I'm not retiring. I saw something.

aka

>duuuuuuuuuuuuude he's still in on LE STHHHHHHECRET


HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHAA

the denial is strong with you boyscouts. but that's because you are....

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62073623
prophetic words here from anon last fall


the cult takes L after L
the spin job gets harder and harder each time


your hubris was your downfall

62073722
>>62070698
He's going to his bunker before shkt hits the fan

62074263
>>62070698
if he steps down now then he can invest now

he won't have conflict of interest

62074346
>>62074263
eh fair enough, either he got canned or left to go all in before the convergence

62076104
>>62074263

62077387
>>62076104

62079182
